blocks.value: Item Response Theory (IRT) models the relationship between a person's latent ability (theta) and the probability that they answer a particular item correctly. Unlike Classical Test Theory, IRT describes each item with its own parameters, so item difficulty and a person's ability are placed on the same scale.

blocks.caption: Item Characteristic Curve: probability of a correct answer rises with ability.

items.q: In IRT, what does the parameter b typically represent?
IRT places item difficulty and person ability on...
The S-shaped curve relating ability to P(correct) is the...

items.back: Item difficulty — the ability level where P(correct)=0.5.
Item discrimination — how sharply the item separates abilities.
Item Characteristic Curve relating ability to P(correct).

blocks.value: IRT links latent ability (theta) to the probability of a correct response. Each item has parameters — difficulty (b) and discrimination (a) — letting items and people sit on one common scale.
